## prompt-1-mermaid

*Generated by Process A from the submitted `prompts/prompt-enactment.md`, adapted
from the prior build's `adoption/sub-prompts/prompt-1-mermaid.md` and the curated
colored Mermaid catalog in `adoption/mermaid/`. This build does not rewrite the
prior adoption framework or review; it produces an analogous, independent set for
the H. R. 9510 passage framework.*

Produce the curated colored Mermaid set for the legislative passage framework into
`law-physical-ai-trials/enactment/mermaid/`. Deliver **20 high-quality,
professional Mermaid figures**, each in its own file under `mermaid/diagrams/` and
each pushed as its own commit, plus a catalog `output-mermaid.md` that embeds all
20 for GitHub rendering and a directory `README.md` naming the upstream files used.

**Palette (strict).** Use only black, grayscales, and the three theme colors
`#4B0082` (indigo, authority, the bill, and the chamber action), `#000080` (navy,
evidence and support), and `#C0C0C0` (silver, cost and caution). The paper template
color is `#4B0082`. Do not introduce any other hue. Fills are the theme colors or
pale gray tints; strokes are black or the deep theme color; text is black on light
fills and white on the `#4B0082` and `#000080` fills. No neon, no gradients, no
cartoon rounded cards (corner radius 2 to 4 pixels only).

**Style discipline (from `adoption/mermaid`).** Crisp near-rectangular boxes;
fluent spline connectors (`basis`, `natural`, `cardinal`, `catmullRom`,
`monotoneX`, `monotoneY`); named `classDef` node classes and container subgraphs so
each diagram scales to a more complex workflow; black text on a white page
background. Use a different and most relevant Mermaid diagram type for each figure
so no two figures repeat the same pattern unnecessarily (flowchart, sequence,
state, class, journey, timeline, quadrant, mindmap, gitGraph, block, sankey,
requirement, and high-complexity maps). Every curved connector that is drawn must
specify its curvature so the TikZ reproduction can carry an explicit looseness.

**Subject re-theming.** Re-theme every diagram to the framework's subject so the
set can illustrate the eight passage-question sections and the bipartisan-passage
argument:

1. The passage decision (a member confronts the bill, runs the eight questions,
   then cosponsors, amends, or declines).
2. The eight passage questions as a legislator's reasoning path from mandate to
   passage.
3. Verification before generation, the legislator's view (the mechanism the bill
   codifies: a system proposes, an independent reviewer checks, ten gates resolve
   ACCEPT, ESCALATE, or BLOCK before a patient).
4. The ten VVUQ gates as the regulatory content Congress is enacting in section
   515D, ending in the three hard catastrophe predicates.
5. The amendment map: new section 515D (21 U.S.C. 360e-5) and the ten conforming
   amendments to Title 21 it carries.
6. The legislative journey: introduction, committee referral, markup, floor vote,
   the second chamber, conference, and enactment.
7. The fiscal picture (authorization 58 million dollars across FY 2027 to 2031,
   outlays 53.5 million dollars, zero net mandatory spending) and the cost
   asymmetry between a small fixed gate cost and an unbounded failure cost.
8. Federalism and the State savings clause, the consensus standards basis feeding
   the gates, constituent reach across the States, the bipartisan framing where an
   innovation appeal and a safety appeal converge on one yes, the stakeholder
   coalition, the platform as proof of implementability, the Senate-companion
   strategy, objection handling, the PAYGO and CutGo scoring path, and the capstone
   from one member's yes to enactment in both chambers.

For every figure, give a one-line title, the Mermaid diagram type, the framework
slot it serves (which of the eight questions, the introduction, the framework, or
an appendix), and a one-line note on why that diagram type is correct for that
content. State that each Markdown Mermaid figure is reproduced in the compiled
LaTeX framework as a matching **colored TikZ** figure in the same palette, and that
these figures are refined across the draft, full, verify, and final stages.

One commit per diagram file (20 commits), then one commit for `output-mermaid.md`
and one for `README.md`. For the second-to-last commit fix all errors across all
files; for the last commit write the directory README (repository structure,
badges, a representative colored Mermaid diagram, table of contents) and refresh
the pull request description. Commit and push in real time; do not hold commits.
Do not stall, ask questions, or go into plan mode.
